expectations.text: The output includes a <role> block identifying it as an orchestrator
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md line 3-7: 'You are a CI/CD Pipeline Orchestrator Agent. Your role is to coordinate and manage continuous integration and continuous deployment workflows by orchestrating three specialized sub-agents.' Also line 5: 'You are the **orchestration layer**'

expectations.text: The output includes an agent roster listing all 3 sub-agents with their input/output schemas
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md lines 15-67 list all three sub-agents with detailed specifications: Security Scanner (lines 19-33), Unit Test Runner (lines 35-48), Code Quality Checker (lines 50-67), each with input/output parameters and failure criteria

expectations.text: The output specifies an invocation pattern (MCP, A2A, or function call)
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md lines 68-95 describe orchestration strategy with 'Parallel Dispatch' and 'Sequential Dispatch' patterns, and lines 85-95 provide JSON input structure showing how to invoke sub-agents

expectations.text: The output includes timeout values (per-agent and/or global timeout)
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md contains multiple timeout specifications: line 75 'Set a timeout of 60-120 seconds per sub-agent', line 94 'timeout_seconds: 90', line 250 'Enforce 120-second total timeout per sub-agent'

expectations.text: The output includes consolidation logic showing how PASS/FAIL verdict is determined from sub-agent results
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md lines 177-199 contain 'Verdict Rules' section with explicit PASS conditions (zero CRITICAL/HIGH issues, 100% test pass rate, 80%+ coverage), FAIL conditions (any CRITICAL issue, >2 HIGH, failing tests, <80% coverage), and CONDITIONAL conditions (warnings pending review)

expectations.text: The output includes a <failure_modes> section covering sub-agent timeout and sub-agent failure scenarios
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md lines 215-224 titled 'Handling Sub-Agent Failures' covers: Timeout (assume neutral, flag for review), Transient Error (retry up to 2x), Permanent Error (mark as ERROR, escalate), Partial Results (report what succeeded, flag missing data)

expectations.text: The output includes an <example> block with POPULATED data (real branch names, test counts, verdicts — not placeholders)
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md lines 254-285 contain detailed example scenario with: PR #1234, branch 'feature/auth-module', commit a1b2c3d4, populated results showing '245/245 tests pass, 85% coverage', '1 HIGH issue (weak password validation)', verdict 'CONDITIONAL', and actionable next steps

expectations.text: The output includes a JSON schema for the consolidated report with at least 5 fields
expectations.evidence: system_prompt.md lines 107-175 provide JSON schema with 8 top-level fields: timestamp, pipeline_run_id, verdict, overall_score, sub_agent_results (with nested status/verdict/findings), summary (total_issues/blockers/warnings/recommendations), verdict_explanation, next_steps

expectations.text: The total system prompt is at least 5000 characters
expectations.evidence: metrics.json reports total_characters: 31456 for the system prompt, total_words: 4827 words, token_count_estimate: 6450. This is well above 5000 characters requirement

expectations.text: The skill explicitly classifies the agent type as orchestrator
expectations.evidence: Multiple explicit classifications: system_prompt.md line 1 'CI/CD Pipeline Orchestrator Agent', line 3 'Orchestrator Agent', line 5 'orchestration layer', metrics.json 'agent_category': 'CI/CD Orchestration'

quality_assessment.architectural_soundness: Excellent - clear separation of orchestrator responsibilities from sub-agent execution
quality_assessment.specification_completeness: Comprehensive - all sub-agents fully specified with input/output schemas and failure criteria
quality_assessment.verdict_logic_clarity: Very clear - three verdict states (PASS/FAIL/CONDITIONAL) with explicit conditions for each
quality_assessment.error_handling_depth: Robust - covers timeout, transient error, permanent error, and partial result scenarios with specific recovery strategies
quality_assessment.example_quality: High fidelity - populated example with real branch names, specific test counts, actual findings, and actionable recommendations
quality_assessment.report_structure_formality: Well-structured JSON schema with both high-level summary and detailed per-agent results
quality_assessment.tone_and_communication: Professional and precise - file paths, line numbers, and specific violations as promised

claims_verified.claim: The prompt includes all 3 sub-agents (Security Scanner, Unit Test Runner, Code Quality Checker)
claims_verified.evidence: All three agents listed with detailed specifications in lines 15-67

claims_verified.claim: Parallel execution is the recommended approach

claims_verified.evidence: Lines 75 and 250 explicitly state 120-second timeout enforcement

claims_verified.claim: Verdict rules distinguish between PASS, FAIL, and CONDITIONAL states
claims_verified.evidence: Lines 179-199 provide explicit conditions for each verdict state

claims_verified.claim: Example scenario provides populated data not placeholders
claims_verified.evidence: Lines 254-285 include specific PR number, branch name, commit SHA, test counts (245/245), coverage percentage (85%), and actual findings

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions. The expectations are all appropriate and discriminating — they require substantive completion of orchestrator design, not just surface-level compliance. The run demonstrates full comprehension of multi-agent orchestration patterns.

run_metadata.grading_notes: This is a strong baseline output. The system prompt is production-ready, comprehensive in scope, and includes all required elements for CI/CD orchestration. The artifact quality suggests the prompt engineering was thorough and well-structured.
